title: spiraea fritschiana var. koreana
intro: Spiraea fritschiana var. koreana, also known as the Korean white spirea, is a deciduous shrub native to Korea and China, characterized by its white or pink flowers and grayish-green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Spiraea fritschiana var. koreana have been linked to several health benefits, including the reduction of fever, relief from headaches and other pains, and the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are typically used in small doses to avoid adverse effects.
